title: uragoga pallens
side_effects: Uragoga pallens can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe gastrointestinal upset, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Prolonged use can also lead to liver and kidney damage, as well as an increased risk of bleeding disorders. In rare cases, improper use of uragoga pallens may cause anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention. Additionally, it may interact with certain medications, exacerbating underlying health conditions and potentially leading to severe complications.
